result:
I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining the illusion that anyone's suffering is “not my problem.”  
Not a sudden flood of empathy that drowns the individual, but an unerasable neural certainty that the pain of any other person is, in mini-dose, my pain—impossible to rationalize away.  
No more invisible refugees, no more “distant” genocides, no more children starving behind statistical curtains.  
The moment your brain tries to file their agony under “someone else,” the circuitry re-labels it “mine,” and the same self-interest that once guarded only your own skin now guards theirs.  
Markets, borders, algorithms, and gods would stay, but every lever of power would be gripped by people who literally cannot profit from harm they feel in their own nerves.  
One generation of that wiring, and cruelty becomes the most expensive luxury on Earth.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
